description:
Your team of automation specialists is designing a tiny command-line “proof-of-concept” workflow that demonstrates how to reshape CSV-style data using only the classic Unix text utilities cut and paste (and a few supporting commands such as echo, cat, tail, etc.).  
    A skeleton data set has already been placed for you under:

        /home/user/source_data/sales_q1.csv
        /home/user/source_data/sales_q2.csv

    1.  Both CSV files have **exactly five columns** in the order  
            Date,Region,Product,Units,Revenue  
        and each file contains one header line plus exactly three data lines.

    2.  Your task is to create a brand-new directory

            /home/user/workflows/quarterly_rollup

        and, inside that directory, generate a single file named

            merged_sales.csv

    3.  The file /home/user/workflows/quarterly_rollup/merged_sales.csv **must contain exactly four lines**:
        • Line-1 is the header  
        • Lines-2,-3,-4 are the data rows

       The precise required contents are:

            Date,Product,Revenue,Q2_Date,Q2_Product,Q2_Revenue
            2024-01-10,Widget,2400,2024-04-11,Widget,2600
            2024-02-12,Gadget,1700,2024-05-15,Gadget,1800
            2024-03-08,Thingamajig,1200,2024-06-18,Thingamabob,1400

       Notes about how this output is produced:
       • From each quarter’s source file, keep only the 1st, 3rd, and 5th columns  
         (Date, Product, Revenue) in that order.  
       • Remove the original header rows before merging.  
       • Merge Q1 and Q2 column triplets **horizontally** so that the three Q1 columns
         are followed by the three Q2 columns, and all six fields are comma-delimited.
       • Finally, prepend the custom header line shown above.

    4.  Also create a plain-text log named

            /home/user/workflows/quarterly_rollup/process.log

        that records the **exact shell commands you executed**, one per line, in the same order
        you ran them.  
        • You may include up to 10 non-blank lines in the log.  
        • Each line should contain nothing except the command itself (no timestamps
          or extra commentary).  
        • The automated checker will replay the commands, so make sure they are
          syntactically correct and strictly necessary for reproducing the final
          merged_sales.csv file.

    5.  Do not move, rename, or modify the original source_data/*.csv files.  
        All output must live only inside /home/user/workflows/quarterly_rollup.

    Summary of what the grader will validate:
        a. Directory /home/user/workflows/quarterly_rollup exists.  
        b. File merged_sales.csv exists and its four lines match the exact text above
           (byte-for-byte, including commas and newlines).  
        c. File process.log exists, has between 1 and 10 non-blank lines, and each
           line is a shell command (no extra commentary).  
        d. No other files are created inside the quarterly_rollup directory.

    You do not need to show the console output here—just perform the steps in the
    terminal so that the required files are present when the grader checks.
truth:
<!-- Existing data files before the task starts -->
    File: /home/user/source_data/sales_q1.csv
    Contents:
        Date,Region,Product,Units,Revenue
        2024-01-10,North,Widget,120,2400
        2024-02-12,South,Gadget,85,1700
        2024-03-08,East,Thingamajig,60,1200

    File: /home/user/source_data/sales_q2.csv
    Contents:
        Date,Region,Product,Units,Revenue
        2024-04-11,North,Widget,130,2600
        2024-05-15,South,Gadget,90,1800
        2024-06-18,East,Thingamabob,70,1400

    -------------------------------------------------------------------
    Expected artefacts AFTER successful task completion
    -------------------------------------------------------------------
    Directory: /home/user/workflows/quarterly_rollup              (mode 755)

    File: /home/user/workflows/quarterly_rollup/merged_sales.csv  (mode 644)
    Exact 4-line content (with a trailing newline after each line):

        Date,Product,Revenue,Q2_Date,Q2_Product,Q2_Revenue
        2024-01-10,Widget,2400,2024-04-11,Widget,2600
        2024-02-12,Gadget,1700,2024-05-15,Gadget,1800
        2024-03-08,Thingamajig,1200,2024-06-18,Thingamabob,1400

    File: /home/user/workflows/quarterly_rollup/process.log       (mode 644)
    • 1–10 non-blank lines, each being a valid shell command.  
    • The grader is agnostic to the specific commands as long as replaying them
      creates exactly the merged_sales.csv file above and nothing else inside the
      quarterly_rollup directory.

    No other files should exist inside /home/user/workflows/quarterly_rollup.
